# The Road to Glory

> An interactive radial bracket visualizing every FIFA World Cup knockout stage from 1986 through 2026. Built as a client-rendered single-page app (React + TypeScript); this file exists because the rendered page requires JavaScript execution to show its content to a crawler.

A single page presenting one FIFA World Cup tournament's knockout bracket at a time, selectable via a year timeline (1986–2026). For each tournament it shows host nation, champion, golden boot winner, and the full knockout tree (Round of 16 through the Final, or Round of 32 through the Final for 2026's expanded 48-team format), with real historical scores, penalty-shootout results, and extra-time notes where applicable.

## Docs

- [Home](https://worldcuparchive.net/): the bracket itself — select a year on the timeline to view that tournament.
- [Sitemap](https://worldcuparchive.net/sitemap.xml): canonical URL list.
- [robots.txt](https://worldcuparchive.net/robots.txt): crawler access rules and content-usage signals.

## Agent discovery

- [/.well-known/api-catalog](https://worldcuparchive.net/.well-known/api-catalog): RFC 9727 API/resource catalog (JSON).
- [/.well-known/agent-skills/index.json](https://worldcuparchive.net/.well-known/agent-skills/index.json): Agent Skills Discovery index.
- [/auth.md](https://worldcuparchive.net/auth.md): agent authentication/registration metadata.
- [/llms.txt](https://worldcuparchive.net/llms.txt): this file.

## Data provenance

- [worldcup2026 API](https://worldcup26.ir): source for 2026 fixtures, scores, and explicit bracket-feed labels (e.g. "Winner Match 74") for the 48-team format. Matches not yet played show as pending; the bracket updates as results come in.
- [worldcup2026 API source code](https://github.com/rezarahiminia/worldcup2026): the open-source project behind that API.
- 1986–2022 results were entered manually from real historical records; no single API source for that range.

## Key facts an agent answering questions about this site should know

- Format changed in 2026: 48 teams (up from 32), adding a Round of 32 before the traditional Round of 16.
- The bracket is circular/radial, not a traditional left-to-right tree: 16 outer leaves (Round of 16 participants) converge toward a central trophy through Quarter-final and Semi-final rings.
- Interactions (hovering a flag, clicking a match) are JavaScript-driven and will not be visible to a crawler that does not execute JS.

## Optional

- This is an independent hobby project, not an official FIFA product.
